content.rendered:
To promote Bruce’s new Wrecking Ball album, Jimmy Fallon declared the last days of February 2012 and the first days of March as “Bruce Springsteen Week,” featuring interviews with and performances by the artist himself.

Bruce did more than debut his own new material, though: he also spoofed himself by donning his 1980s uniform (and hair!) and joining Jimmy Fallon-as-Neil Young for an inspired cover of LMFAO’s “Sexy and I Know It.”

Here’s that brilliantly silly performance:

Sexy and I Know It
First performed: March 2, 2012 (New York City, NY)
Last performed: March 2, 2012 (New York City, NY)

© March 24, 2018

excerpt.rendered: A self-spoofing Bruce joins Jimmy Fallon-as-Neil Young for a hilarious take on LMFAO’s “Sexy and I Know It.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Whip My Hair
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce and Jimmy Fallon (as Neil Young) perform the unlikeliest of covers in one of Bruce's most viral moments. Watch the performance and backstory inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR/RotD: Bruce Springsteen and Jimmy Fallon: Gov. Christie Traffic Jam
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: At the height of the Bridgegate scandal, Bruce reprised his 80s-era team-up with Jimmy Fallon to throw some shade Governor Christie's way.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: November 16
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce debuts "Sugarland," plays the first of two amazing Christic shows, and releases The Promise: The Darkness on the Edge of Town Story on this remarkable date. Lots more highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Chinatown
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: At the height of the pandemic, Bruce came out of lockdown to appear on Jack Antonoff's album track, video, and live rooftop performance.

jetpack-related-posts.title: You Sexy Thing
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It doesn't get steamier than two Bruces teaming up for an accordion-powered duet on Hot Chocolate's 1975 surprise hit, "You Sexy Thing."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: November 2
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: "Working on a Dream" makes its debut, Bruce headlines a Hurricane Sandy telethon, and the first of several Seeger Sessions. More highlights inside.
